items.title: INTERNATIONAL SPACE STATION
items.summary: Boeing awarded ~$22.4B by NASA for the International Space Station.

items.title: IGF::CL,CT::IGF MANAGEMENT AND OPERATION OF THE NATIONAL SECURITY CAMPUS -- CONTRACT NO. DE-NA0002839
items.summary: Honeywell Federal Manufacturing & Technologies (subsidiary of Honeywell) awarded ~$17.4B by DOE for management and operation of the National Security Campus.

items.title: TAS::80 0124::TAS DESIGN, DEVELOPMENT, TEST&EVALUATION OF PROJECT ORION
items.summary: NASA Orion spacecraft design, development, test & evaluation contract worth ~$15.6B.

items.title: MANAGEMENT AND OPERATION OF THE NAVAL NUCLEAR LABORATORY AND NAVAL NUCLEAR PROPULSION PROGRAM SUPPORT
items.summary: DOE contract (~$14.8B) for management and operation of the Naval Nuclear Laboratory and Naval Nuclear Propulsion Program support.

items.title: PROVIDE DEVELOPMENTAL HARDWARE AND TEST ARTICLES, AND MANUFACTURE AND ASSEMBLE ARES I UPPER STAGES. THE UPPER STAGE (US)
items.summary: NASA contract (~$10.5B) to provide developmental hardware, test articles, and manufacture/assemble Ares I Upper Stages.

items.title: FIRST DDT AND E, ARES I-X, AND FLIGHT TESTS.  FIRST STAGE WILL BE A FIVE SEGMENT, SOLID ROCKET BOOSTER DERIVED FROM THE
items.summary: NASA contract (~$4.4B) for Ares I-X first-stage DDT&E and flight tests using a five-segment solid rocket booster derived from the Space Shuttle SRB.

items.title: IGF::CT::IGF  RS-25 PRODUCTION RESTART TO BE UNDERTAKEN BY THE CONTRACTOR IN SUPPORT OF PROVIDING SIX RS-25 ENGINES MODI
items.summary: L3Harris (via Aerojet Rocketdyne) awarded ~$3.27B by NASA for RS-25 engine production restart to supply six engines for the Space Launch System.

items.title: SCIENCE OPERATION AND MAINTENANCE SUPPORT FOR THE UNITED STATES ANTARCTIC PROGRAM
items.summary: Leidos awarded ~$3.09B by the National Science Foundation for science operation and maintenance support of the U.S. Antarctic Program.

items.title: SPACE LAUNCH SYSTEM (SLS) STAGES PRODUCTION AND EVOLUTION CONTRACT (SPEC)
items.summary: Boeing awarded ~$2.53B by NASA for Space Launch System (SLS) Stages production and evolution.

items.title: THE PURPOSE OF THIS DELIVERY ORDER AWARD IS TO ADD FUNDING FOR FTI TELECOMMUNICATIONS SERVICES.
items.summary: L3Harris (formerly Harris Corporation) received funding from the Department of Transportation for FTI telecommunications services.

items.title: IGF::OT::IGF NEXTSTEP NRA AWARD.  THIS NEXTSTEP CONTRACT BUILDS UPON THE SUCCESS OF COMMERCIAL ORBITAL TRANSPORTATION SE
items.summary: Northrop Grumman won a NASA NextSTEP contract building on commercial orbital transportation services for deep space exploration.

items.title: DAFIS UDO RECONSTRUCT W/O ADVANCE
items.summary: L3Harris (formerly Harris Corporation) received Department of Transportation funding for DAFIS telecommunications services.

items.title: GLOBAL SECURITY ENGINEERING&SUPPLY CHAIN SERVICES.
items.summary: General Dynamics Information Technology won a ~$1.55B Department of State contract for global security engineering and supply chain services.

items.title: EXTRAVEHICULAR SPACE OPERATIONS CONTRACT (ESOC)
items.recipient: HAMILTON SUNDSTRAND SPACE SYSTEMS INTERNATIONAL, INC.
items.summary: Hamilton Sundstrand Space Systems (Collins Aerospace/RTX) won a ~$1.45B NASA contract for extravehicular space operations (ESOC).

items.title: SOFTWARE LIFE CYCLE DEVELOPMENT
items.summary: Science Applications International Corporation won a ~$1.43B GSA contract for software life cycle development.

items.title: TAS::75 0140::TAS CONSTRUCTION OF VACCINE MANUFACTURING FACILITY
items.agency: Department of Health and Human Services
items.summary: Seqirus (CSL Limited subsidiary) won a ~$1.43B HHS contract for construction of a vaccine manufacturing facility.

items.title: EHRM WAVES K, L, M (VISN 23) AND N, O (VISN 15) DEPLOYMENTS
items.summary: Oracle Health Government Services won a ~$1.42B Department of Veterans Affairs contract for EHRM wave deployments across VISNs 23 and 15.

items.title: OFFSHORE PATROL CUTTER (OPC) STAGE 2 CONTRACT: DETAILED DESIGN AND PRODUCTION (DD&P) OF 11 OPCS.
items.summary: Austal USA (Austal Limited, ASX: ASB) won a ~$1.40B DHS contract for detailed design and production of 11 Offshore Patrol Cutters.

items.title: Financial Data Transparency Act Joint Data Standards
items.agency: Treasury Department, Comptroller of the Currency, Federal Reserve System, Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation, National Credit Union Administration, Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, Federal Housing Finance Agency, Commodity Futures Trading Commission, Securities and Exchange Commission
items.summary: Joint financial regulator rule establishing interoperable data standards under the Financial Data Transparency Act; imposes new compliance/technology costs on large financial institutions but may reduce long-term reporting friction across agencies.

items.title: Tanker Security Program
items.summary: MARAD finalizes the Tanker Security Program, providing federal incentive payments to qualifying U.S.-flag tanker operators who make vessels available for national security activation—direct revenue support for participating tanker shipping companies.

items.title: Application for Authorization To Transmit Electric Energy to a Foreign Country
items.summary: DOE finalizes the application process for authorization to transmit electric energy to foreign countries, affecting utilities and power marketers engaged in cross-border electricity trade with Canada and Mexico; streamlining could ease export approvals but adds procedural requirements.

items.title: Fee Schedules; Fee Recovery for Fiscal Year 2026
items.summary: NRC FY2026 fee schedules raise licensing, inspection, and annual fees on nuclear licensees to recover costs per NEIMA, increasing operating costs for nuclear plant operators.

items.title: Implementation of the Substance Use-Disorder Prevention That Promotes Opioid Recovery and Treatment for Patients and Communities Act of 2018: Dispensing and Administering Controlled Substances for Medication-Assisted Treatment
items.summary: DEA finalizes SUPPORT Act rules expanding conditions practitioners must meet to provide medication-assisted treatment for opioid use disorder—could broaden patient access to MAT drugs but adds compliance requirements for prescribers.

items.title: Federal Independent Dispute Resolution Operations
items.agency: Personnel Management Office, Treasury Department, Internal Revenue Service, Labor Department, Employee Benefits Security Administration, Health and Human Services Department
items.summary: Federal IDR process rules under the No Surprises Act finalize new requirements for dispute resolution between insurers and out-of-network providers, affecting claims costs and reimbursement dynamics for health insurers and PBMs.

items.title: Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standard No. 214; Side Impact Protection; Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standard No. 305a; Electric-Powered Vehicles: Electric Powertrain Integrity; Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standard No. 307; Fuel System Integrity of Hydrogen Vehicles
items.agency: Transportation Department, National Highway Traffic Safety Administration
items.summary: NHTSA removes obsolete requirements from FMVSS side-impact, electric powertrain integrity, and hydrogen fuel system standards, modestly reducing regulatory compliance burden for automakers including EV manufacturers.

items.title: National Emission Standards for Hazardous Air Pollutants From Hazardous Waste Combustors: Residual Risk and Technology Review
items.summary: EPA finalizes residual risk and technology review for hazardous air pollutant emissions from hazardous waste combustors, likely requiring additional emissions controls and compliance spending for commercial hazardous waste combustion facilities.

items.title: Medicare Program; Alternative Payment Model Updates and the Increasing Organ Transplant Access (IOTA) Model
items.agency: Health and Human Services Department, Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services
items.summary: CMS finalizes IOTA Model updates for organ transplant access, tying payment to transplant outcomes; could shift more kidney patients toward transplant and away from long-term dialysis, pressuring dialysis providers' revenue while mixed for managed-care/ transplant service networks.

items.title: Phasedown of Hydrofluorocarbons: Reconsideration of Certain Regulatory Requirements Promulgated Under the Technology Transitions Provisions of the American Innovation and Manufacturing Act of 2020
items.summary: EPA reconsiders and modifies HFC technology-transition restrictions under the AIM Act; changes to phasedown requirements affect chemical producers like Chemours and alternative-refrigerant suppliers like Honeywell, with direction-dependent upside or downside.

items.title: Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act, HHS Notice of Benefit and Payment Parameters for 2027; and Basic Health Program
items.agency: Health and Human Services Department, Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services,
items.summary: HHS finalizes ACA benefit and payment parameters for 2027, including risk-adjustment and user-fee changes for marketplace insurers; directly shapes economics for exchange-participating health insurers.

items.title: Implementing PATRIOT Act Improvements: Contraband Cigarettes and Smokeless Tobacco
items.agency: Justice Department, Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives Bureau
items.summary: ATF finalizes rules implementing PATRIOT Act provisions on contraband cigarette/smokeless tobacco trafficking, tightening enforcement against illicit trade and benefiting legitimate tobacco manufacturers and distributors by reducing grey-market competition.

items.title: Updated Staple Food Stocking Standards for Retailers in the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program
items.agency: Agriculture Department, Food and Nutrition Service
items.summary: USDA codifies new staple food stocking-variety framework for SNAP retailer eligibility, altering compliance requirements for grocery and discount retailers that depend on SNAP redemptions.

items.title: Defense Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplement: Disclosure of Greenhouse Gas Emissions (DFARS Case 2024-D021)
items.agency: Defense Department, Defense Acquisition Regulations System
items.summary: DoD final rule requires defense contractors to disclose greenhouse gas emissions under FY2024 NDAA authority, adding compliance obligations for prime contractors bidding on federal work.

items.title: Small Business Lending Under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (Regulation B)
items.summary: CFPB revises Section 1071 small-business lending data collection rules under Regulation B, imposing revised compliance and reporting burdens on banks and lenders that extend credit to small businesses.
